rulings.Case Title: NANCY MACHADO et al VS. AMERICAN ASBESTOS CO., et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 1, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT UNION OIL COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 14, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD ENNEY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 1, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT YARWAY CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF UNDER ANDREWS. THE MCCLAREN DECLARATION IS ADMITTED OVER DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS AND CREATES TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT GIVEN THE TESTIMONY OF MAXWELL CONFIRMING THE USE OF THE ASBESTOS IN DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NIESAR & WHYTE LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. VACAVILLE FORD-MERCURY, INC., A DELAWARE et al

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ROJAS VS. SAN FRANCISCO EXAMINER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 1, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANT THE S..F. NEWSPAPER COMPANY, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FORTY-FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PARAGRAPHS 20 - 22, 30 - 33, 42 - 46 AND 50 - 53 ALLEGE COLLECTIVE ACTION PROTECTED BY THE N.L.R.A. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE CONDUCT THAT IS OUTSIDE OF THE PARAMETERS OF THE N.L.R.A. AND/OR TO ALLEGE THAT THE N.L.R.B. HAS DECLINED TO CONSIDER THE MATTER. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: HOUSEHOLD FINANCE CORPORATION OF CALIFORNIA VS. DAVID K UTHMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 1, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANT DAVID UTHMAN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ACGUISTAPACE VS. GEORGE PADGETT et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 1, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANT GEORGE PADGETT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. ALTHOUGH THE COURT MAY TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE FACT THAT THE PARTIES SETTLED THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION, THE CONTENTS OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ARE HEARSAY AND THE COURT MAY NOT TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE TRUTH OF THE TERMS. KILROY V STATE (2004) 119 CAL APP 4TH 140, 145. TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANTS SEEK TO ENFORCE THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT, THERE ARE WAYS TO DO THAT APART FROM A DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: THAI MORELAND VS. NINH THI NGUYEN
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 1, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT NINH NGUYEN'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT WAS SERVED WITH THE COMPLAINT IN THE NEW YORK ACTION BUT THE COURT LACKED PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. THE ONLY CONTACT DEFENDANT HAD WITH NEW YORK IS DESCRIBED AS "A SERIES OF PHONE CALLS" TO PLAINTIFF IN NEW YORK. IN ADDITION, THE PARTIES AGREED THAT THE TRANSACTION IS GOVERNED BY NEW YORK LAW AND PAYMENTS ON THE NOTE ARE TO BE MADE IN NEW YORK. ALL OF THE OTHER CONTRACTS OCCUR IN CALIFORNIA. THE NOTE IS SIGNED IN CALIFORNIA, THE PROCEEDS ARE USED IN CALIFORNIA AND PLAINTIFF'S FUNDS ARE TRANSFERRED FROM HER NEW YORK ACCOUNT TO DEFENDANT'S ACCOUNT IN CALIFORNIA. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DECISION IN GLASS V HARRIS (1988) 687 F. SUPP. 906 TO BE CONTROLLING AND IN SO RULING, THE COURT NOTES THE EMPHASIS PLACED ON THE DEPOSIT OF FUNDS IN NEW YORK IN THE CASE OF CATALYST ENERGY DEVELOPMENT CORP. V IRON MTN. MINES, INC. 630 F. SUPP. 1314. =(302/PJM/PB)
